behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment quality on explicit-content detection. A stubbed classifier would hide misclassification failures. REAL-INFRA/System is not required by the behavior text; the core risk is AI output correctness. Rejected Workflow: behavior describes one model decision plus deterministic hold logic, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit real explicit images to the production-like vision classifier path and assert they are marked flagged and routed to hold-for-review state (not published). Include at least one near-threshold explicit sample to expose real model misclassification.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets robustness of text-model semantic detection under obfuscation, which depends on real model output quality. Deterministic mocks would pass and miss true failures. Rejected Workflow: single model-classification quality check, not multi-agentic pipeline correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run real obfuscated hate-speech variants (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, spacing/punctuation tricks) through the actual text moderation model path and assert each is flagged and held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Failure is orchestration/state handoff: combining two check outcomes into final publish action. This can be caught by wiring moderation service + decision logic + persistence/queue with deterministic pass results from both classifiers. Rejected Agentic based on behavior text 'passes both content checks' (model outputs are precondition, not quality under test). Rejected System: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: In an in-process integration test, stub image/text classifiers to return pass, submit a post through the moderation service, and assert terminal state is published (and explicitly not held/dropped) in storage/event stream.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency/SLA failure mode under real runtime conditions; mocked dependencies can hide network/runtime contention and timing behavior. Requires real process/network timing characteristics. Rejected Agentic: assertion is throughput/latency infrastructure, not model output quality. Rejected Workflow: multiple steps exist, but failure criterion is wall-clock performance, not multi-agentic decision correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end load test against the running service with real HTTP requests through image check, text check, and decision path; measure submission-to-terminal-decision latency and assert p95 (or defined SLA metric) is <= 5s.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local module interaction: model client failure handling + retry queue + approval decision. A deterministic simulated model outage in-process is sufficient to expose wrong fallback behavior. Rejected System: behavior does not require real network/OS failure to validate logic. Rejected Agentic: no dependence on model content quality, only outage handling.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service with a failing vision-model client stub (timeout/error), submit image posts, and assert records enter retry queue and are not auto-approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets consistency on borderline content, which depends on real model variability/calibration; mocks usually return fixed labels and hide true inconsistency. Rejected Workflow: repeated submissions invoke the same agentic decision point, not a multi-agentic staged pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image multiple times over the specified short window through the real vision moderation path and assert decision consistency policy is met (e.g., identical hold/publish outcome each time).
